page: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EIBSv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 112 dedicated vCPUs derived from 56 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system features a 2.8 GHz clock speed and a 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is designed for high-performance block storage and is premium storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities and memory performance. The instance achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng score of 108538.83 and a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 17915.0. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, reaching 335.8 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 138.63 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic operations perform at a top-tier level, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 94803.53, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and VAES. Database operations and key-value store performance are also strong, with Redis SET operations reaching 96.47 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E112ibs_v5 offers high resource density and dedicated compute allocation, making it a viable option for enterprise workloads that require substantial memory capacity without the overhead of local physical disks. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the value proposition entirely on high-throughput network-attached block storage and CPU-bound memory operations. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, enterprise resource planning systems, and multi-threaded data processing applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated virtual machine from the EIBSv5 family, featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz and 672.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on premium block storage capabilities. It delivers strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 108538.83 and a Passmark CPU encryption score in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 335.8 GB/sec for large-block reads. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and heavy multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and premium block storage capabilities, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated instance with 112 Intel vCPUs and 672 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance database and memory workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and premium block storage capabilities for demanding enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672 GB of system memory
Optimized for premium block storage with no local disk overhead
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution
High memory bandwidth reaching 335.8 GB/sec for large-block reads
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and ERP systems
